title: SELECT
summary: Retrieve columns and expressions from one or more tables.

common: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE active = TRUE ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;
tsql: SELECT TOP (20) id, name FROM users WHERE active = 1 ORDER BY created_at DESC;

notes: Projection order affects output column order.
Without ORDER BY, row order is not guaranteed.

notes.tsql: Use TOP instead of LIMIT for row limiting.

title: FROM
summary: Specify the table, view, or subquery data source.
notes: Use aliases for readability in multi-join queries.

title: WHERE
summary: Filter rows before grouping and aggregation.

common: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE total > 100 AND status = 'paid';

notes: Predicates can enable index seeks when sargable.

title: GROUP BY
summary: Group rows for aggregate calculations.

common: SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;

notes: Every selected non-aggregate column must be grouped.

title: HAVING
summary: Filter grouped rows after aggregation.

common: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) c FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

notes: Use WHERE for pre-aggregation filters when possible.

title: ORDER BY
summary: Sort rows by one or more expressions.

common: SELECT id, created_at FROM events ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC;

notes: Include deterministic tie-breakers for stable pagination.

title: LIMIT
summary: Return at most N rows.

common: SELECT * FROM logs ORDER BY ts DESC LIMIT 50;
tsql: SELECT TOP (50) * FROM logs ORDER BY ts DESC;

notes: Use with ORDER BY for deterministic results.

title: OFFSET
summary: Skip N rows before returning results.

tsql: ORDER BY expression OFFSET row_count ROWS FETCH NEXT page_size ROWS ONLY

common: SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id LIMIT 20 OFFSET 40;
tsql: SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id OFFSET 40 ROWS FETCH NEXT 20 ROWS ONLY;

notes: Large offsets are expensive; prefer keyset pagination for deep pages.

title: JOIN
summary: Combine rows from two datasets using a join condition.

common: SELECT u.name, o.total FROM users u JOIN orders o ON o.user_id = u.id;

notes: Missing predicates can create accidental cartesian products.

title: UNION
summary: Combine result sets, removing duplicates.

common: SELECT email FROM customers UNION SELECT email FROM leads;

notes: Use UNION ALL to avoid duplicate elimination sort/hash cost.

title: INTERSECT
summary: Return rows present in both result sets.

mysql: SELECT ... FROM (...) a INNER JOIN (...) b USING (...) /* emulate INTERSECT */

common: SELECT id FROM active_users INTERSECT SELECT id FROM newsletter_subscribers;
mysql: SELECT a.id FROM active_users a JOIN newsletter_subscribers n ON n.id = a.id;

notes: MySQL requires emulation in versions without INTERSECT support.

title: EXCEPT
summary: Return rows from first query not in second query.

mysql: SELECT ... FROM a WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM b WHERE ... ) /* emulate EXCEPT */

common: SELECT id FROM users EXCEPT SELECT user_id FROM bans;
mysql: SELECT u.id FROM users u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM bans b WHERE b.user_id = u.id);

notes: SQL Server synonym: EXCEPT; Oracle uses MINUS.

title: WITH
summary: Define one or more Common Table Expressions (CTEs).

common: WITH recent_orders AS (SELECT * FROM orders WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days') SELECT COUNT(*) FROM recent_orders;

notes: CTEs improve readability; optimization behavior differs by engine/version.

title: WITH RECURSIVE
summary: Build recursive queries for trees, graphs, and sequences.

common: WITH RECURSIVE nums(n) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT n + 1 FROM nums WHERE n < 5) SELECT * FROM nums;
tsql: WITH nums(n) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT n + 1 FROM nums WHERE n < 5) SELECT * FROM nums OPTION (MAXRECURSION 100);

notes: Always define a termination condition.
Check recursion limits in your engine.

title: CREATE TABLE
summary: Create a new table with columns and constraints.

common: CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE, created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL);

notes: Use explicit constraints for data integrity and query planner hints.

title: ALTER TABLE
summary: Modify table schema (columns, constraints, defaults).

sqlite: ALTER TABLE table_name RENAME TO new_name
ALTER TABLE table_name ADD COLUMN column_def

common: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN last_login TIMESTAMP;
sqlite: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN last_login TEXT;

notes: SQLite supports fewer ALTER variants than other engines.

title: TRANSACTIONS
summary: Group statements into an atomic unit of work.

common: BEGIN; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE id = 1; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 100 WHERE id = 2; COMMIT;

notes: Pick proper isolation level to balance consistency and concurrency.

title: LIKE
summary: Pattern match using % and _ wildcards.

common: SELECT * FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%@example.com';

notes: Leading wildcard prevents normal index usage in many engines.

title: ILIKE
summary: Case-insensitive LIKE (PostgreSQL).

sqlite: expression LIKE pattern /* often case-insensitive depending on collation */
tsql: expression LIKE pattern /* use case-insensitive collation */

postgres: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name ILIKE 'jo%';
mysql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE LOWER(name) LIKE LOWER('jo%');
sqlite: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name LIKE 'jo%';
tsql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name COLLATE Latin1_General_CI_AS LIKE 'jo%';

notes: Native ILIKE is PostgreSQL-specific.

title: EXISTS
summary: Test whether a subquery returns any row.

common: SELECT * FROM users u WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id);

notes: Often more efficient than IN for correlated predicates.

title: IN
summary: Match against a list or subquery result set.

common: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status IN ('paid', 'shipped');

notes: NULLs in subquery can affect NOT IN semantics.

title: BETWEEN
summary: Inclusive range comparison.

common: SELECT * FROM events WHERE ts BETWEEN '2025-01-01' AND '2025-01-31';

notes: For timestamps, end-exclusive ranges are often safer.

title: JSON Operators
summary: Access fields in JSON documents.
notes: Operator and function names vary a lot by dialect.

title: INTEGER
summary: Whole-number type for ids, counters, and discrete values.
notes: Check signed range limits per engine.

title: DECIMAL
summary: Exact fixed-point numeric type for money and precision-sensitive values.
notes: Prefer DECIMAL over FLOAT for financial values.

title: BOOLEAN
summary: Truth value type with dialect-specific representations.
notes: Literal and storage representations differ across engines.

title: TIMESTAMP
summary: Date and time type for event moments and audit columns.
notes: Timezone behavior differs between TIMESTAMP variants by engine.
